allow:
Bash(1 <<'EOF'
import frappe
import importlib.util
import os

frappe.init\(site="university.local"\)
frappe.connect\(\)
frappe.set_user\("student@test.edu"\)

# Load exams.py the way Frappe does
print\("=== Testing exams.py loading ==="\)
exams_path = "apps/university_erp/university_erp/www/student-portal/exams.py"

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location\("exams_module", exams_path\)
exams_module = importlib.util.module_from_spec\(spec\)

# Import frappe into the module namespace \(required for the module to work\)
exams_module.frappe = frappe
exams_module._ = frappe._

try:
    spec.loader.exec_module\(exams_module\)
    print\("✅ Module loaded successfully"\)
    print\("Has get_context:", hasattr\(exams_module, 'get_context'\)\)
    print\("Has get_current_student:", hasattr\(exams_module, 'get_current_student'\)\)
    
    # Try calling get_context
    context = frappe._dict\(\)
    result = exams_module.get_context\(context\)
    print\("\\n✅ get_context\(\) executed successfully!"\)
    print\("Active page:", context.get\('active_page'\)\)
    print\("Has student:", 'student' in context\)
    print\("Has stats:", 'stats' in context\)
    if 'stats' in context:
        print\("Stats:", context.stats\)
        
except Exception as e:
    print\("❌ Error:", str\(e\)\)
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc\(\)
EOF)
